DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-09-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JENNIFER A. LIPSKI, Esq. of JML Law (Tel: 818-610-8800) for plaintiff;
Atty. JOSHUA D. KIENITZ, Esq. of Littler Mendelson, P.C. (Tel: 925-932-2468) for defendants PG&E Corporation and Pacific Gas and Electric Company.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS PG&E CORPORATION AND PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court rules on the demurrers to plaintiff's first amended complaint (FAC) as follows:

Count 1: The FAC adequately pled violation of Labor Code 1102.5, so the demurrer is overruled. The word "unknown" appears nowhere in the statute and the "reasonable cause to believe" is the employee's. (Dem. 2:25; Lab. Code 1102.5.) The FAC pled violations of specific laws. (FAC 4:16-18.) Defendants' notion that a heightened pleading standard exists is unsupported by the summary judgment case they cite. (Dem. 2:25-28.)
Count 5: Plaintiff agrees to dismiss this cause of action, so the demurrer is moot.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-16-2021 Continued To Mar-09-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
